note: storytree's real roadmap, as a dependency map — not a timeline. Each node is a piece of the thing; an arrow means 'this had to come first, and it unlocks that'. Milestones are gates: real-world conditions (people, backing) that must be true before the work behind them is worth building. Edit this file to change what the roadmap shows.

nodes.title: Proof gates
nodes.short: Nothing ships without proof — a failing test made to pass for the right reason, then a person signs off.

nodes.title: The living map
nodes.short: Every project is a tree of stories you can watch grow, instead of a plan hidden in someone's head.

nodes.title: Many builders, in parallel
nodes.short: People and AI agents build different parts of the tree at once — a control room, with humans in charge of what's true.

nodes.title: The open record
nodes.short: Decisions, reasoning and the work itself kept on a shared record you can read — the foundation the trust model stands on.

nodes.title: A record even we can't quietly edit
nodes.short: Making the record append-only and tamper-evident, so the people running storytree can't rewrite history unseen. The foundation already exists.

nodes.title: Ask the project anything
nodes.short: A way to question the project in plain language, against its whole record — so a hidden agenda has nowhere left to hide.

nodes.title: A real circle of members
nodes.condition: People beyond the founder are actually building here.
nodes.short: We won't pretend at community before there is one. Shared decisions only mean something once there are people to share them with.

nodes.title: Your voice becomes your vote
nodes.short: Today one person decides what gets built next. This turns 'your voice counts' into a real say — once there are members whose say it is.

nodes.title: Open the source
nodes.short: Open source once the record is trustworthy enough and there's a circle ready to steward it — not before it's ready to be.

nodes.title: It can sustain itself
nodes.condition: Real users, and the backing to keep going.
nodes.short: Governance is not worth building for an experiment of one with no users and no backing. Reach this first; then it earns the right to grow.

nodes.title: Real governance
nodes.short: Who eventually holds these rules, and how they change — built for a project that exists at scale, not promised into a vacuum.
